summary: The implementation of the CSV file upload and parsing functionality meets the specified requirements. All necessary files are present, and the core functionality is correctly implemented, including robust error handling and database interactions.

criteria.name: Completeness
criteria.description: Check all files listed in the instructions are present in the result.
criteria.details: All required files (financeRoutes.js, financeController.js, csvParser.js, Transaction.js, db.js, app.js, and test files) are present.

criteria.name: Correctness
criteria.description: Verify each function/class does what the instructions specify.
criteria.details: The CSV upload endpoint, parsing service, and database model are implemented correctly. The uploadCsv function handles file uploads, parsing, and storage as specified. The csvParser service correctly parses CSV files and handles errors.

criteria.name: Quality
criteria.description: Check for well-structured code with proper types and error handling.
criteria.details: The code is well-structured with comprehensive error handling, including custom error classes for CSV parsing errors.

criteria.name: Safety
criteria.description: Check for forbidden paths, path traversal, or hardcoded secrets.
criteria.details: No forbidden paths, path traversal, or hardcoded secrets were detected. File paths are handled safely.
